channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google monopoly: Prosecutors hint at company breakup, but analysts say it’s unlikely
item.description: The case was filed in 2020, heralding a new U.S. government legal effort to rein in tech giants, with anti-monopoly cases against Amazon, Apple, and Facebook and Instagram parent firm Meta.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Politics of Patriotism: How America's Imagery Became a Right-Wing Calling Card
item.description: Exploring the intersection of patriotism and politics in America, where the right tends to dominate online spaces and the American flag has become a symbol of Trumpism.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok was aware of risks kids and teens face on its platform, legal document alleges
item.description: TikTok has been aware that its design features are detrimental to its young users and that publicly touted tools aimed at limiting kids’ time on the site were largely ineffective.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: House Democrats press tech leaders to curb hurricane misinformation
item.description: A group of House Democrats representing areas affected by hurricanes Helene and Milton are calling on the leaders of major technology companies to do more to combat the flurry of misinformation online about the extreme weather. In a letter sent Friday, Reps. Deborah Ross (N.C.), Kathy Castor (Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The inside story of Roku’s TV program
item.description: Ten years ago, streaming stick maker Roku released its first smart TVs in partnership with Hisense and TCL, two Chinese TV manufacturers that few had heard of in the U.S. at that time. In the years since, Roku has become the domestic smart TV market leader.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: IBM: 78% of mainframe shops are piloting, operating AI apps
item.description: AI is reinvigorating the mainframe and causing enterprises to rethink their plans for mainframe modernization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trials for 2019 kidnapping, killing of Santa Cruz tech entrepreneur divided into four
item.description: Attorneys have argued in court filings that a fair trial was not possible because several of the defendants implicated each other during post-arrest interviews with police detectives, and that transcript redactions would be difficult to untangle for each.
item.dc:creator: Jessica York, The Mercury News

item.title: Five days a week in the office? Forget it!
item.description: A few weeks ago, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that all corporate employees, including AWS staffers, will be expected to work in the office five days a week beginning in January. I don’t work for Amazon, but if I did, I’d quit. Period. End of statement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez promises 'brawl' if 'billionaires' force out Lina Khan
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) vowed Wednesday there would be a “brawl” if wealthy Democratic donors attempt to force Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan out of a potential Harris administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft brings Copilot AI tools to OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot is now available in OneDrive, part of a wider revamp of the company’s cloud storage platform. Microsoft highlighted several ways to use the generative AI (genAI) assistant in OneDrive in a blog post Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sanders: Mark Cuban wrong to call for Lina Khan to be replaced
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) publicly disagreed with Mark Cuban after he called for Federal Trade Commissioner (FTC) Chair Lina Khan to be replaced if Vice President Harris is elected president. In a post on the social platform X, Sanders said Cuban “is wrong” when he called for a new FTC chair.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Linus Torvalds annoyed by passive language in commit messages
item.description: The latest release candidate of Linux 6.12 was announced over the weekend, promoting some comments about grammar from Linux founder Linus Torvalds.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft wants Copilot to be your new AI best friend
item.description: “It’s great to see you!” Microsoft’s Copilot AI chatbot underwent a transformation last week, morphing into a simplified pastel-toned experience that encourages you…to just chat.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Databricks says with its new Databricks Apps platform, you can build tailored enterprise apps in 5 minutes
item.description: Every enterprise today is looking to get as much as possible out of its data and AI investments. But when building in-house apps, developers can struggle with infrastructure constraints and maintenance, security, data governance, compliance, and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The high-speed clay technique shaking up home construction
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how Clay’s impact printing technology enables fast, sustainable construction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rust resumes rise in popularity
item.description: Rust, the fast and memory-safe programming language born out of Mozilla, has resumed its rise up the monthly Tiobe index of programming language popularity, although it still has not cracked the index’s top 10.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The best new features and fixes in Python 3.13
item.description: Python 3.13 has just been released. This article presents a rundown of the most significant new features in Python 3.13 and what they mean for Python developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AirPods 4 vs Pro 2: Is the newer model worth it?
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson compares the AirPods 4 and the AirPods Pro 2. "For me, audio quality is the most essential factor in buying new headphones or earbuds," Kurt says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
